transcript.text: Hello, hello, living in Boulder.
Vlog with Kyle Pounds for the 19th of December 2013.
It's currently the 20th at 4.36 in the morning.
I guess I had some, I must have had a nap, I think, for a little bit today.
This morning, I got up.
I was supposed to work at 9, but they postponed it till 10.
And then they postponed it till Monday.
And so all day long, I just, I was working here on my audio book page.
And I didn't convert the amount of words of all the books to the number, to a number of pages and hours.
So I've been making that conversion and doing other stuff that I don't want to talk about, because that's another video.
So, all day long on that page.
And in the afternoon, my roommate, my roommate's, like, roommate for a long time, like, friend.
I've known, actually, for a long time.
I'm in third grade with him.
Went to high school.
Came over and hung out, because he's living with his mom.
A couple buildings away, he got flushed out of his place from the flood.
And hanging out for a couple, few hours.
First time I hung out with somebody for a long time.
And, uh, then I started working here again, listening to YouTube videos of, uh, high school, uh, teachers giving lessons on, like, the Thirty Warriors War and stuff.
And, uh, I didn't ride my bike or anything today.
I, a lot, most of the day I was, I was typing on my calculator, doing the conversion, so I couldn't have really been riding the bike today anyways.
I didn't have the, um, motivation today anyways.
I was kind of, I don't know, I guess taking, just focusing so much on the, um, audio book page really, um, threw me.
But I'll do that tomorrow.
Um, got, really, got another cold front today, so maybe that'll motivate me to stay inside.
I, I accidentally fed my, uh, geckos too much today.
I was like, I just had my little cylinder down to tap some, uh, crickets in there.
And then the gecko went and attacked the cylinder.
Because you saw there were bugs up there, crickets.
So I was like, whoa, whoa, whoa, that freaked me out.
And I shook, like, 50 of them out in there.
So they're gonna pig out.
I saw a video yesterday, some kid was saying if you feed your geckos when they're a little too much, they can die.
They can overeat and stuff, so.
Uh, but no, they always, they're always up to eat, so anyways.
Uh, I figure I can, I can feed, I figure I can feed them a lot because, um, I get a thousand crickets.
I don't know if I can keep a thousand crickets alive that long in there.
It'll be interesting.
So I might as well just feed my geckos then.
And let them hang out.
Make it like a real environment.
But anyways, so that's that.
Uh, tomorrow it'll be on the audiobook all day long and, uh, and, uh, riding on the bike.
I need to make sure my, uh, electricity bill is paid, though, and I need a vacuum tomorrow.
So I'll keep you guys posted on those details.
Peace out.
